text.title: devops-aws-terraform-cicd Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for devops-aws-terraform-cicd. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Terraform projects MUST use per-environment directories (infrastructure/environments/dev, /staging, /prod). Each environment MUST have its own main.tf, variables.tf, and terraform.tfvars. State backend MUST use S3 with a DynamoDB lock table. Provider default_tags MUST include Project, Environment, and ManagedBy="terraform" applied to all resources.
rule.rationale: Per-environment directories ensure that a Terraform error in dev cannot affect prod state. S3+DynamoDB backend prevents concurrent apply runs from corrupting state. Default_tags ensure every resource is tagged for cost allocation without per-resource tag blocks.

rule.statement: GitHub Actions workflows for Terraform MUST use aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials with role-to-assume (OIDC), never with aws-access-key-id/aws-secret-access-key. The apply job MUST use max-parallel: 1 in the matrix strategy to prevent concurrent applies. The prod environment MUST be protected with a required reviewer in GitHub Environment settings.
rule.rationale: OIDC eliminates long-lived AWS credentials from GitHub Secrets, reducing the blast radius of a secret leak to the duration of one token (15 minutes). max-parallel=1 prevents race conditions between environment applies sharing the same state bucket. Required reviewer for prod ensures a human reviews the plan before production infrastructure changes.
